revision.text:
{{Buddhism}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[宮島]]の[[弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）に祀られる全国唯一の[[鬼神]]。別名[[厳島]]三鬼坊
== 由来 == [[弘法大師]][[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に[[弥山]]を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
== 概要 == 三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で[[不動明王]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]]・[[権現]] *[[大聖院]] *[[弥山]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]]

{{Buddhism}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[宮島]]の[[弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）に祀られる全国唯一の[[鬼神]]。別名[[厳島]]三鬼坊
== 由来 == [[弘法大師]][[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に[[弥山]]を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
== 概要 == 三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で[[不動明王]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ウン ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]]・[[権現]] *[[大聖院]] *[[弥山]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]]

魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で[[不動明王]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ウン ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]]・[[権現]] *[[天狗]] *[[大聖院]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]]

{{改名提案|[[三鬼権現]]|date=2010年8月}} {{Buddhism}} '''三鬼権現'''（さんきごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[宮島]]の[[弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）に祀られる全国唯一の[[鬼神]]。別名[[厳島]]三鬼坊。

追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る[[鬼神]]で[[不動明王]]を[[本地仏]]とする。

{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:鬼|*]] [[Category:天狗|*]]

{{改名提案|[[三鬼権現]]|date=2010年8月}} {{Buddhism}} '''三鬼権現'''（さんきごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[宮島]]の[[弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる[[鬼神]]。別名[[厳島]]三鬼坊。
== 由来 == [[弘法大師]][[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。

追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ウン ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]]・[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[大聖院]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:鬼|*]] [[Category:天狗|*]]

{{改名提案|[[三鬼権現]]|date=2010年8月}} {{Buddhism}} '''三鬼権現'''（さんきごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる[[鬼神]]。別名、厳島三鬼坊。
== 由来 == 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
== 概要 == 三鬼権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ウン ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]]・[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[大聖院]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:鬼|*]] [[Category:天狗|*]]

{{Buddhism}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる[[鬼神]]。別名、厳島三鬼坊。
全国的に見ても鬼神自体（別な神を鬼神に見立てている訳で無く）を祀られる例はほぼ無く、[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]では全国唯一と称されている。（あくまでも大聖院での見解）
また、勧請先でも三鬼大権現として祀られているのが確認出来ており、三鬼権現と呼ばれる事は極めて少ないと思われる。
== 由来 == 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。

追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ウン ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]]・[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[大聖院]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:鬼|*]] [[Category:天狗|*]]

{{Buddhism}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる[[鬼神]]。別名、厳島三鬼坊。

追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。

{{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:鬼|*]] [[Category:天狗|*]] {{Buddhism-stub}}

また、勧請先でも三鬼大権現として祀られているのが確認出来ており、三鬼権現と呼ばれる事は極めて少ないと思われる。

== 概要 == 三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。
魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
== 真言 == オン アラタンノウ ウン ソワカ
== 関連項目 == *[[神仏習合]] *[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[大聖院]] *[[三瀧寺]]

魔羅鬼神（まらきしん） 「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。

== 概要 == 三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、地元では三鬼さんと親しまれている。初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん） 「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。
時眉鬼神（じびきしん） 「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。

revision.text:
{{Buddhism}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、[[真言宗]]系の天狗信仰や[[山岳仏教]]の信仰対象となる[[鬼神]]である。別名、厳島三鬼坊。
== 概要 == 主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる。三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、地元では三鬼さんと親しまれている。初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
*追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん）：「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。 *時眉鬼神（じびきしん）：「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。※ *魔羅鬼神（まらきしん）：「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。 （※[[弥山本堂]]にはその虚空蔵菩薩が[[本尊]]として祀られている。）
[[神仏分離令]]により元々祀られていた[[御山神社]]（みやまじんじゃ）から現在の三鬼堂へ遷された経緯がある。
==== 由来 ==== 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
==== 真言 ==== オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ
また、三鬼大権現の功徳[[和讃]]も存在する。
== 関連項目 == *[[仏の一覧]] *[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[山岳信仰]] *[[三瀧寺]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さんきたいこんけん}} [[Category:真言系仏教]] [[Category:密教]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:三神]] [[Category:神仏習合]] [[Category:鬼|*]] [[Category:天狗|*]]

'''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、[[真言宗]]系の天狗信仰や[[山岳仏教]]の信仰対象となる[[邪神#神道の邪神|鬼神]]である。別名、厳島三鬼坊。
== 概要 == 主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる。三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、地元では三鬼さんと親しまれている。初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
*追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん）：「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。 *時眉鬼神（じびきしん）：「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。※ *魔羅鬼神（まらきしん）：「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。 （※[[弥山本堂]]にはその虚空蔵菩薩が[[本尊]]として祀られている。）
[[神仏分離令]]により元々祀られていた[[御山神社]]（みやまじんじゃ）から現在の三鬼堂へ遷された経緯がある。
==== 由来 ==== 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
==== 真言 ==== オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ
また、三鬼大権現の功徳[[和讃]]も存在する。

=== 由来 === 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
=== 真言 === オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ
また、三鬼大権現の功徳[[和讃]]も存在する。
== 関連項目 == *[[仏の一覧]] *[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[山岳信仰]] *[[三瀧寺]] *[[狗賓]]

'''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、[[真言宗]]系の天狗信仰や[[山岳仏教]]の信仰対象となる[[鬼神]]である。別名、厳島三鬼坊。
== 概要 == 主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）等に祀られる。三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、地元では三鬼さんとして親しまれている。初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も篤く信仰したといわれる。
* 追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん）：「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。 * 時眉鬼神（じびきしん）：「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする。※ * 魔羅鬼神（まらきしん）：「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
（※[[弥山本堂]]にはその虚空蔵菩薩が[[本尊]]として祀られている。）
[[神仏分離令]]により元々祀られていた[[御山神社]]（みやまじんじゃ）から現在の三鬼堂へ遷された経緯がある。
=== 由来 === 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]し祀ったのが始まりとされる。
=== 真言 === オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ
また、三鬼大権現の功徳[[和讃]]も存在する。
== 関連項目 == *[[仏の一覧]] *[[権現]] *[[大天狗]] *[[山岳信仰]] *[[三瀧寺]] *[[狗賓]]
{{密教2}} {{Buddhism2}} {{神道 横}}

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2022-01-20}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、[[真言宗]]系の天狗信仰や[[山岳仏教]]の信仰対象となる[[鬼神]]である。別名は'''厳島三鬼坊'''。
== 概要 == 主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）などに祀られる。三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、地元では三鬼さんとして親しまれている。初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も深く信仰したといわれる。
* 追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん）：「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。 * 時眉鬼神（じびきしん）：「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする（[[弥山本堂]]にはその虚空蔵菩薩が[[本尊]]として祀られている）。 * 魔羅鬼神（まらきしん）：「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
[[神仏分離令]]により、元々祀られていた[[御山神社]]（みやまじんじゃ）から現在の三鬼堂へ遷された経緯がある。
=== 由来 === 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]して祀ったのが、始まりとされる。
=== 真言 === 「オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ」。また、三鬼大権現の功徳[[和讃]]も存在する。
== 関連項目 == * [[仏の一覧]] * [[権現]] * [[大天狗]] * [[山岳信仰]] * [[三瀧寺]] * [[狗賓]]

{{出典の明記|date=2022-01-20}} '''三鬼大権現'''（さんきだいごんげん）は、[[真言宗]]系の天狗信仰や[[山岳仏教]]の信仰対象となる[[鬼神]]である。別名は'''厳島三鬼坊'''。
== 概要 == 主に[[広島県]]廿日市市[[厳島|宮島]]の[[弥山 (広島県)|弥山]]の三鬼堂と、同島の[[大聖院 (宮島)|大聖院]]内魔尼殿（まにでん）などに祀られる。三鬼大権現は大小の[[天狗]]を眷属に従え、強大な神通力で衆生を救うとされ、地元では三鬼さんとして親しまれている。初代[[総理大臣]]の[[伊藤博文]]も深く信仰したといわれる。
* 追帳鬼神（ついちょうきしん）：「福徳」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[大日如来]]を[[本地仏]]とする。 * 時眉鬼神（じびきしん）：「知恵」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[虚空蔵菩薩]]を本地仏とする（[[弥山本堂]]にはその虚空蔵菩薩が[[本尊]]として祀られている）。 * 魔羅鬼神（まらきしん）：「降伏」の徳を司る鬼神で、[[不動明王]]を本地仏とする。
[[神仏分離令]]により、元々祀られていた[[御山神社]]（みやまじんじゃ）から現在の三鬼堂へ遷された経緯がある。
=== 由来 === 弘法大師[[空海]]が大同元年(806年)に弥山を開基した時、三鬼大権現を[[勧請]]して祀ったのが、始まりとされる。
=== 真言 === 「オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ」。また、三鬼大権現の功徳[[和讃]]も存在する。

== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.

== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

revision.text:
== Sanki Daigongen == '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * **Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)**: A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * **Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)**: A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * **Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)**: A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.

== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

'''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).

Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.

Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen.

'''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|ja|弥山 (広島県)}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|ja|大聖院 (宮島)}} on {{ill|Miyajima|ja|宮島}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|ja|廿日市市}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Dainichi Nyorai|ja|大日如来}} as the {{ill|honji-butsu|ja|本地仏}} (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Kokūzō Bosatsu|ja|虚空蔵菩薩}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓}}
{{Buddhism2}} {{Shinto-Buddhism}} {{Shinto-Buddhism-stub}}

Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=draft}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=draft}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃|12=draft}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現|12=draft}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=draft}}

'''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a {{ill|demon deity|ja|鬼神|12=draft}} revered in {{ill|Tengu|ja|天狗|12=draft}} worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教|12=draft}}, particularly within the {{ill|Shingon Buddhism|ja|真言宗|12=draft}} tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).

Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|ja|伊藤博文|12=draft}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.

Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=draft}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Fudō Myōō|ja|不動明王|12=draft}} as the honji-butsu.

=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|wasan|ja|和讃|12=draft}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Gongen|ja|権現|12=draft}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|ja|山岳信仰|12=draft}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|ja|三瀧寺|12=draft}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=draft}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三鬼大権現]] {{Infobox deity|venerated_in={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|qid=Q550182}} |associated_deities ={{ill|Acala|qid=Q337624}}; {{ill|Vairocana|qid=Q239847}}; {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|qid=Q416174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11358323]] Wikidata name: Sanki Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a [[:en:Gwisin|demon deity]] revered in [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain Buddhism|qq=|1=Mountain Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467855}}, particularly within the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki-dō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maniden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in]] on [[:en:Miyajima|Miyajima]], located in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] as the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|qq=|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "qid=" to "WD="
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三鬼大権現]] {{Infobox deity|venerated_in={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}} |associated_deities ={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}; {{ill|Vairocana|WD=Q239847}}; {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11358323]] Wikidata name: Sanki Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a [[:en:Gwisin|demon deity]] revered in [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain Buddhism|qq=|1=Mountain Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467855}}, particularly within the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki-dō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maniden|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in]] on [[:en:Miyajima|Miyajima]], located in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] as the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|qq=|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

It is said that [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] as the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] as the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.

=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三鬼大権現]] {{Infobox deity|venerated_in={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}} |associated_deities ={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}; {{ill|Vairocana|WD=Q239847}}; {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358323]] Wikidata name: Sanki Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a [[:en:Gwisin|demon deity]] revered in [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain Buddhism|1=Mountain Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467855}}, particularly within the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maniden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in]] on [[:en:Miyajima|Miyajima]], located in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] as the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三鬼大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三鬼大権現]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:三鬼大権現]] {{Infobox deity|venerated_in={{ill|Shingon Buddhism|WD=Q550182}} |associated_deities ={{ill|Acala|WD=Q337624}}; {{ill|Vairocana|WD=Q239847}}; {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11358323]] Wikidata name: Sanki Daigongen [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:05, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Sanki Daigongen''' (三鬼大権現) is a [[:en:Gwisin|demon deity]] revered in [[:en:Tengu|Tengu]] worship and {{ill|mountain Buddhism|ja|山岳仏教|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/mountain Buddhism|1=Mountain Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11467855}}, particularly within the [[:en:Shingon Buddhism|Shingon Buddhism]] tradition. It is also known as '''Itsukushima Sanki-bō''' (厳島三鬼坊).
== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on [[:en:Mount Misen|Mount Misen]] and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maniden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Daishō-in|Daishō-in]] on [[:en:Miyajima|Miyajima]], located in [[:en:Hatsukaichi|Hatsukaichi]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]]. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that [[:en:Itō Hirobumi|Itō Hirobumi]], Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with [[:en:Vairocana|Dainichi Nyorai]] as the [[:en:Honji suijaku|Honji Buddha]] (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with [[:en:Ākāśagarbha|Kokūzō Bosatsu]] as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with [[:en:Acala|Fudō Myōō]] as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are [[:en:Wasan|wasan]] (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * [[:en:Gongen|Gongen]] * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * [[:en:Mountain worship|Mountain worship]] * [[:en:Mitaki-dera|Mitaki-dera]] * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

== Overview == Sanki Daigongen is primarily enshrined at the {{ill|Sanki-dō|ja|三鬼堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanki-dō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} on {{ill|Mount Misen|en|Mount Misen|WD=Q3109237|lt=Mount Misen}} and within the {{ill|Maniden|ja|魔尼殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Maniden|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Daishō-in|en|Daishō-in|WD=Q3012169|lt=Daishō-in}} on {{ill|Miyajima|en|Miyajima|WD=Q6884207|lt=Miyajima}}, located in {{ill|Hatsukaichi|en|Hatsukaichi|WD=Q736897|lt=Hatsukaichi}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}} as the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|WD=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha}} (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|Wasan|en|Wasan|WD=Q11418206|lt=wasan}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * {{ill|Gongen|en|Gongen|WD=Q3080343|lt=Gongen}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=Mountain worship}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en|Mitaki-dera|WD=Q63712|lt=Mitaki-dera}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|Wasan|en|Wasan|WD=Q11418206|lt=wasan}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * {{ill|Gongen|en|Gongen|WD=Q3080343|lt=Gongen}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=Mountain worship}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en|Mitaki-dera|WD=Q63712|lt=Mitaki-dera}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}

Sanki Daigongen is believed to command both great and small Tengu as retainers, using vast supernatural powers to save sentient beings. Locally, it is affectionately referred to as '''Sanki-san'''. It is said that {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|WD=Q174971|lt=Itō Hirobumi}}, Japan’s first Prime Minister, was a devout follower.
The three deities of Sanki Daigongen are: * '''Tsuichō Kishin (追帳鬼神)''': A deity governing the virtue of "fortune and virtue" (福徳), with {{ill|Vairocana|en|Vairocana|WD=Q239847|lt=Dainichi Nyorai}} as the {{ill|Honji suijaku|en|Honji suijaku|WD=Q3080781|lt=Honji Buddha}} (original Buddha). * '''Jibi Kishin (時眉鬼神)''': A deity overseeing the virtue of "wisdom" (知恵), with {{ill|Ākāśagarbha|en|Ākāśagarbha|WD=Q416174|lt=Kokūzō Bosatsu}} as the honji-butsu. Kokūzō Bosatsu is also the principal deity enshrined at {{ill|Misen Main Hall|ja|弥山本堂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Misen Main Hall|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * '''Mara Kishin (魔羅鬼神)''': A deity associated with the virtue of "subjugation" (降伏), with {{ill|Acala|en|Acala|WD=Q337624|lt=Fudō Myōō}} as the honji-butsu.
Following the {{ill|Shinbutsu Bunri|ja|神仏分離令|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinbutsu Bunri|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神仏分離|1=shinbutsu bunri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1970535}} (the separation of Shinto and Buddhism), the deities were relocated from the original {{ill|Miyama Shrine|ja|御山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyama Shrine|qq=}} to the current Sanki-dō.
=== Origin === It is believed that the revered monk {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} (also known as Kōbō Daishi) enshrined Sanki Daigongen in 806 CE (Daido 1) when he founded Mount Misen as a sacred site.
=== Mantra === The mantra associated with Sanki Daigongen is: * '''On Aratan Nō Un Sowaka''' (オン・アラタンノウ・ウン・ソワカ).
Additionally, there are {{ill|Wasan|en|Wasan|WD=Q11418206|lt=wasan}} (Buddhist hymns) praising the virtues of Sanki Daigongen. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Buddhas|ja|仏の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Buddhas|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 日本の仏尊|1=Japanese Buddhist pantheon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6158131}} * {{ill|Gongen|en|Gongen|WD=Q3080343|lt=Gongen}} * {{ill|Daitengu|ja|大天狗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daitengu|qq=}} * {{ill|Mountain worship|en|Mountain worship|WD=Q93697439|lt=Mountain worship}} * {{ill|Mitaki-dera|en|Mitaki-dera|WD=Q63712|lt=Mitaki-dera}} * {{ill|Guhin|ja|狗賓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Guhin|1=guhin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11571397}}
